enriched_points.expanded_content: Rodents, particularly mice and rats, are extensively used due to their genetic similarity to humans, short life cycles, and ease of handling for assessing nanotoxicity.
Zebrafish are popular in nanotoxicology studies because of their transparent embryos, rapid development, and ability to model human diseases, providing insights into aquatic toxicity.
Other organisms, such as fruit flies and worms, serve as alternative models. They offer reduced ethical concerns while enabling the study of basic biological processes affected by nanoparticles.
Key criteria include relevance to human biology, ease of manipulation, ethical considerations, and the ability to provide meaningful data on nanoparticle effects and mechanisms.
